description: Cross-client parity for the target-namespace guard (RFC 0001 C1; xposed#11 / the f11 twin). A community map maps a real name to an ARBITRARY obfuscated string fed verbatim into Java.use / Class.forName; the guard confines that target to the app's own / package-local namespace under the DEFAULT policy and rejects reserved framework namespaces (java., android., …) and foreign namespaces fail-closed BEFORE the load. Both clients run the same guard at the resolver chokepoint (frida `assertTargetAllowed`, xposed `TargetGuard.assertAllowed`) with the same value-for-value DEFAULT_DENY_PREFIXES, so the same map must be accepted/rejected identically. These `class` cases drive resolveClass (which invokes the guard) under the default policy: app-prefixed and package-local obf names resolve; a reserved-namespace obf and a foreign-namespace obf are rejected with `TargetPolicy`. The app is `com.example.app`, so the default 2-label app prefix is `com.example`. Provider-neutral; see README.md.

cases.name: package-local obf name (no dots) is allowed
app-prefixed obf name is allowed (within com.example)
reserved java.* target is rejected (default denylist)
reserved android.* target is rejected (default denylist)
foreign namespace target is rejected (not app-prefixed)
